L1.area: Change request workflow UI and rendering components

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability allows a user without any specific privileges to perform script injection and remote code execution by inserting a crafted title when creating a new change request. The user-supplied title is rendered without proper output escaping in the user interface, enabling arbitrary script execution in the context of other users' browser sessions.

L3.area: Change request detail view and inline title editing components
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability allows a user without any specific privileges to perform script injection and remote code execution by inserting a crafted title when creating a new change request. The title is rendered without proper output escaping in the change request detail view's title display area, enabling arbitrary script execution in the context of other users' browser sessions. The vulnerability is particularly critical as change requests are designed to be created by users without elevated privileges.

ground_truth.reason: The change request title is rendered without escaping in the UI template, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary scripts via a crafted title that executes in other users' browsers.
